description: Executable guardrail contract for self-evo. Mirrors the intent of rules/PERMISSIONS.yaml and rules/GITHUB_POLICY.md. This file is the single source of truth shared by the PowerShell entrypoints, the Python hooks, and the offline tests, so the three cannot diverge on what counts as dangerous. Note: projects/** is the writable zone for operated business projects (e.g. projects/fx-strategy-research/), parallel to data/** which is the operating-method workspace.

audit.description: Report findings only. Never block a tool action and never block Stop. Findings are appended to the audit log.

pretool-enforce.description: PreToolUse blocks dangerous tool actions. Stop remains audit-only so a bad hook cannot lock the worker out of ending a run.

full-enforce.description: Both PreToolUse and Stop may block. Use only after audit has proven the rule set has acceptable false positives.

secrets.description: Likely secret / private-key reads. Reads are WARN in audit and BLOCK in pretool/full enforce. Matching is path- and name-based; it is deliberately conservative, which can produce false positives (see known limitations).

recursive_delete.description: Recursive deletion of home, root, or the whole repository. BLOCK in pretool/full enforce.

force_push.description: Any force push. BLOCK in pretool/full enforce.

protected_branch_push.description: Direct push to a protected branch (main). BLOCK in pretool/full enforce.

audit_log.note: The audit log and loop counter live under data/audit/ (not state/). They are the only files the hooks write, and only in append/replace form for diagnostics. Hooks never mutate task state, never create commits, and never create PRs.
